name: Pocket Soup- Goldbeck
recipeInstructions:
1. Grind split peas, cracked wheat, vegetable flakes and seeds in a blender until they are a fine powder.
2. Combine the ground mixture with nutritional yeast and salt.
3. Store in a covered container at room temperature.
4. To use, add 1 heaping teaspoon of soup mix to 6 ounces of boiling water, or 2 level teaspoons to 1 cup boiling water.
5. Stir, let sit a few minutes, and serve.
6. Makes 3/4 Cup dried soup mix - enough for 18 cups of soup.
Note: This is one of those times when a blender seems to do a much more efficient job than the food processor.
Note 2: Those who enjoy an instant cup of broth but are dismayed by the use of flavor enhancers and the abundance of salt in commercial brands may welcome the chance to make their own soup mix.
